title: A method for transforming 3-dimensional image data into a 2-dimensional image
abstract: A method for transforming 3-dimensional image data into a 2-dimensional image comprising the following steps: providing a 3-dimensional model of an object containing image data of surface of said object in the form of a multiplicity of surface data units; determining a pivot point internally to surface of said object; configuring a cylindrically shaped grid of endpoints around said object; for calibration purposes, associating each of said endpoints with one of said surface data units, such that each of said endpoints is associated with one of said surface data units on a line between said pivot point and said endpoint; mapping image data in said multiplicity of surface data units and extracting the image data onto said cylindrically shaped grid of endpoints, such that each endpoint represents the image data of its associated surface data unit to form a graphical representation of the image data on surface of said object on said cylindrically shaped grid of endpoints; transforming image data on said cylindrically shaped grid of endpoints into a 2-dimensional image.
claims:
Claims 1. A method for transforming 3 -dimensional image data into a 2-dimensional image comprising the following steps: (i) providing a 3 -dimensional model of an object containing image data of surface of said object in the form of a multiplicity of surface data units; (ii) determining a pivot point internally to surface of said object; (iii) configuring a cylindrically shaped grid of endpoints around said object; (iv) for calibration purposes, associating each of said endpoints with one of said surface data units, such that each of said endpoints is associated with one of said surface data units on a line between said pivot point and said endpoint; (v) mapping image data in said multiplicity of surface data units and extracting the image data onto said cylindrically shaped grid of endpoints, such that each endpoint represents the image data of its associated surface data unit to form a graphical representation of the image data on surface of said object on said cylindrically shaped grid of endpoints; (vi) transforming image data on said cylindrically shaped grid of endpoints into a 2- dimensional image. 2. A method for transforming 3 -dimensional image data into a 2-dimensional image comprising the following steps: (i) providing a 3 -dimensional model of an object containing image data of surface of said object in the form of a multiplicity of surface data units; (ii) determining a pivot point internally to surface of said object; (iii) configuring a sphere shaped grid of endpoints around said object; (iv) for calibration purposes, associating each of said endpoints with one of said 

 surface data units, such that each of said endpoints is associated with one of said surface data units on a line between said pivot point and said endpoint; (v) mapping image data in said multiplicity of surface data units and extracting the image data onto said sphere shaped grid of endpoints, such that each endpoint represents the image data of its associated surface data unit to form a graphical representation of the image data on surface of said object on said sphere shaped grid of endpoints; (vi) transforming image data on said sphere shaped grid of endpoints into a 2- dimensional image. 3. A method for transforming 3 -dimensional image data into a 2-dimensional image comprising the following steps: (i) providing a 3 -dimensional model of an object containing image data of surface of said object in the form of a multiplicity of surface data units; (ii) configuring a cylindrically shaped grid of endpoints around said object; (iii) determining an axis of pivot points internally to surface of said object and in parallel to said cylindrically shaped grid of endpoints; (iv) for calibration purposes, associating each of said endpoints with one of said surface data units, such that each of said endpoints is associated with one of said surface data units on a line between said endpoint and the pivot point on said axis of pivot points closest to said endpoint; (v) mapping image data in said multiplicity of surface data units and extracting the image data onto said cylindrically shaped grid of endpoints, such that each endpoint represents the image data of its associated surface data unit to form a graphical representation of the image data on surface of said object on said cylindrically shaped grid of endpoints; 

 (vi) transforming image data on said cylindrically shaped grid of endpoints into a 2- dimensional image. 4. A method for transforming 3 -dimensional image data into a 2-dimensional image comprising the following steps: (i) providing a 3 -dimensional model of an object containing image data of surface of said object in the form of a multiplicity of surface data units; (ii) configuring a sphere shaped grid of endpoints around said object; (iii) determining an axis of pivot points internally to surface of said object and in parallel to said sphere shaped grid of endpoints; (iv) for calibration purposes, associating each of said endpoints with one of said surface data units, such that each of said endpoints is associated with one of said surface data units on a line between said endpoint and the pivot point on said axis of pivot points closest to said endpoint; (v) mapping image data in said multiplicity of surface data units and extracting the image data onto said sphere shaped grid of endpoints, such that each endpoint represents the image data of its associated surface data unit to form a graphical representation of the image data on surface of said object on said sphere shaped grid of endpoints; (vi) transforming image data on said sphere shaped grid of endpoints into a 2- dimensional image. 5. The method of claim 1, 2, 3 or 4, wherein said 3-dimensional model is arranged in the form of voxels. 6. The method of claim 1, 2, 3 or 4, wherein said 3-dimensional model is arranged in the form of texels. 7. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein position of said pivot point is determined in 

 center of said object. 8. The method of claim 3 or 4, wherein position of said axis of pivot points is determined in center axis of said object. 9. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein position of said pivot point is determined on a point that corresponds to the average of minimum and maximum values of said object on X, Y and Z axes. 10. The method of claim 3 or 4, wherein position of said axis of pivot points is determined on an axis that corresponds to the average of minimum and maximum values of said object on X and Z axes. 11. The method of claim 1, 2, 3 or 4, wherein the image data on said 2-dimensional image is printed on sheets that can be flexibly adjusted to cover the surface of a 3- dimensional physical object. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein said sheets are adhered onto surface of a 3- dimensional physical object. 13. The method of claim 1, 2, 3 or 4, wherein the image data on said 2-dimensional image is further processed to fill in areas with missing image data based on image data in adjacent areas. 14. The method of claim 1, 2, 3 or 4, wherein the image data on said 2-dimensional image is further processed to refine image data on said 2-dimensional image. 15. The method of claim 2 or 4, wherein the image data on said 2-dimensional image is further processed to complement areas of missing image data formed during transformation of image data on said sphere shaped grid of endpoints into a 2- dimensional image. 16. The method of claim 1, 2, 3 or 4, wherein the image data on said 2-dimensional image is further processed to refine image data on said 2-dimensional image based on 

 attributes of said object in said 3 -dimensional model, such that the distribution of color on a printed sheet based on said 2-dimensional image conforms to the designated distribution of color on surface of a 3 -dimensional physical object onto which said printed sheet is flexibly adjusted. 17. The method of claim 1 or 3, wherein said cylindrically shaped grid of endpoints is split into two half cylindrically shaped grids of endpoints, such that steps (iii) to (vi) are performed twice, each time in respect to another grid of endpoints of said two half cylindrically shaped grids of endpoints. 18. The method of claim 2 or 4, wherein said sphere shaped grid of endpoints is split into two half sphere shaped grids of endpoints, such that steps (iii) to (vi) are performed twice, each time in respect to another grid of endpoints of said two half sphere shaped grids of endpoints. 19. The method of claim 1 or 3, wherein said cylindrically shaped grid of endpoints is oval. 20. The method of claim 2 or 4, wherein said sphere shaped grid of endpoints is oval. 21. The method of claim 1 or 3, wherein steps (iii) to (vi) are performed twice in a first cycle and a second cycle, such that in said second cycle said cylindrically shaped grid of endpoints is reconfigured perpendicularly to the position said cylindrically shaped grid of endpoints configured during said first cycle, thereby any of said surface data units of said object not mapped during first cycle are mapped during said second cycle. 22. The method of claim 21, wherein said 2-dimensional image generated in said first cycle and said 2-dimensional image generated in said second cycle are integrated to generate a comprehensive 2-dimensional image representing image data on all of said surface data units on said object.
